ops.title: Fast-lane concurrency hardening: same-session races, eviction gap, concurrent-serve harness
ops.description: Consolidated from rounds 3-4 of the adversarial review (codex xhigh; all prompt-correctness invariants PASS — these are one-bust cache-economics exposures on concurrent same-session requests, all pre-existing in kind, watched by the realized-bust breaker). (1) Stale-eligibility-snapshot reopen: a request's (floor, window_closed) snapshot can predate a sibling's close_window/verbatim forward; on completion its stash_egress_snapshot unconditionally reopens the window and its recode can diverge from what the sibling sent (variants exist with no kill/shadow involved — two ordinary Forwards racing, and a staged-plan take() vs fresh-commit race). Candidate fixes per review: a close-generation/epoch check so a pre-close snapshot cannot reopen, or same-session serialization from eligibility-read through forward. (2) Eviction-between-lookups: a control-plane eviction mid-flight orphans the Arc<SessionEcon> a request snapshotted; its forward can diverge once; the replacement econ is born closed so state self-heals. (3) Test debt: no concurrent serve() harness exists — the named races have no regression coverage; kill/shadow-before-lazy-init was claimed as a gap but is REFUTED by born-closed semantics (lazily created econ starts window_closed=true, floor 0). Add the harness alongside any fix.

ops.text: pre-close eligibility snapshot cannot reopen the window (epoch check or serialization)
concurrent-serve regression harness covers the recorded races
